rulings.Case Title: ATHECIA SANCHEZ VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion (Opening Brief) For Writ Of Mandate To Enforce The California Public Records Act
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Friday, April 24, 2026, Line 12, PETITIONER ATHECIA SANCHEZ's Motion For Writ Of Mandate To Enforce The California Public Records Act. Hearing required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JISOO YOO ET AL VS. POST-MONTGOMERY ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNAIA GENERAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Compliance With Ccp Section 2031.280(A)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Friday, April 24, 2026, Line 11, PLAINTIFFS JISOO YOO and JULIE YOO's Motion To Compel Defendant's Compliance With CCP Section 2031.280(A). Plaintiffs' motion to compel defendant's compliance with CCP Section 2031.280(a) is denied. Both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are also denied. Other than a brief discussion of one blank page in their reply memorandum, nowhere in plaintiffs' moving or reply papers do plaintiffs explain the need for the document identification that they request in this motion. The court is left with the distinct impression that plaintiffs' counsel aim is to impose "busy work" on the defendant's counsel that will provide little or no benefit to plaintiffs. Defendant's reliance on its responsive separate statement on plaintiffs' earlier motion to compel to argue that it has provided the identification that plaintiffs seek is also questionable. While that document identifies some documents that were produced in response to some of the document requests, it does not make any identification of documents to several of the requests. The time and money spent on this battle is much better spent on litigating issues that matter. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA HARRINGTON ET AL VS. ALEJANDRA GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Court'S March 9, 2026, Order And For Monetary Sanctions As To Plaintiff Linda Harrington And Her Attorney Of Record, Dylan Hackett
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Friday, April 24, 2026, Line 6, DEFENDANT ALEJANDRA GARCIA's Motion To Enforce The Court'S March 9, 2026, Order And For Monetary Sanctions As To Plaintiff Linda Harrington And Her Attorney Of Record, Dylan Hackett. Defendant Alejandra Garcia's motion to enforce the March 9, 2026 order and for monetary sanctions is denied. Plaintiff Linda Harrington's request for sanctions is denied. This motion, much less the reply papers, should never have been filed. The opposition papers should have been a single page. It appears that the motion arises out of the distaste that counsel have for one another and their desire to litigate that distaste in court. While the court cannot require that counsel like each other, the court can and does require adherence to the rules of civility. The rush to file this motion should not have been necessary. The request for additional sanctions is punitive and contrary to the spirit of the discovery statutes. The court does not know whether Harrington's counsel would have paid the sanctions order less quickly but for this motion, but that certainly seems possible based on the papers submitted. Due to the shortness of life and the desire to avoid further motions such as this one, neither side is awarded sanctions on this motion. Counsel on both sides should be able to find ways to spend their time more productively than filing and opposing this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT STEWART VS. ERICA PYCH KURIEN D.O.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Friday, April 24, 2026, Line 8, 1- DEFENDANT ERICA KURIEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Erica Kurien's motion to strike the references to punitive damages from plaintiff Robert Stewart's complaint is granted without leave to amend unless and until the plaintiff obtains an order per CCP 425.13 allowing him to file an amended complaint seeking punitive damages. Plaintiff alleges that the defendant unlawfully recorded plaintiff's confidential medical appointments without consent and maintained false documents stating that plaintiff consented to having his appointments recorded. Defendant's motion to strike is limited to the three references to punitive damages in the complaint at paragraphs 3, 57, and subsection (d) of the prayer for relief. Plaintiff must comply with the CCP 425.13 before seeking punitive damages because his claim "arises out of the professional negligence of a health care provider." (Central Pathology Service Med. Clinic Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 3 Cal.4th 181; Davis v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 623.) Plaintiff's claims are directly related to and arise out of the procedures followed by his health care provider during the course of treatment. The court does not address plaintiff's request for trial preference in his opposition memorandum. A request for trial preference must be made by a separately filed motion and noticed to be heard in department 206. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ABBETT ELECTRIC CORPORATION VS. TUTOR PERINI CORPORATION ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Friday, April 24, 2026, Line 9, DEFENDANT TUTOR PERINI CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Tutor Perini Corporation's demurrer to the fifth and sixth causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Abbett Electric Corporation is sustained with 15 calendar days leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for fraud in the inducement and sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Friday, April 24, 2026, Line 10, DEFENDANT ALEXANDER GLAVIN'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS.
